text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Raymond Kowalski
MRN: [REDACTED]
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE]
DATE OF REPORT: [DATE]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [PATHOLOGIST NAME, MD]
CLINICIAN: [ONCOLOGIST NAME, MD] — Medical Oncology
CLINICAL HISTORY: 71-year-old male with known metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC), Gleason grade group 5 adenocarcinoma, germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant (c.5946delT, p.Ser1982Argfs*22), previously treated with ADT/abi followed by Xtandi. Now with progressive disease on Xtandi per PSMA PET/CT (new and enlarging osseous metastases, enlarged right obturator lymph node). CT-guided biopsy performed to confirm histology, assess for neuroendocrine transdifferentiation, and provide tissue for molecular profiling prior to consideration of PARP inhibitor therapy.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN IDENTIFIER
  Specimen A: CT-guided core needle biopsy, right iliac crest osseous lesion
  Specimen received: Two (2) formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) core needle biopsy cylinders
  Specimen designation: A1 (labeled "right iliac crest bone biopsy, core 1"), A2 (labeled "right iliac crest bone biopsy, core 2")
  Fixative: 10% neutral buffered formalin
  Decalcification: Yes (formic acid-based decalcification performed prior to processing)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSIS

SPECIMEN A — CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY, RIGHT ILIAC CREST:

  METASTATIC PROSTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA
  - Gleason grade group 5 (Gleason score 9, pattern 4+5)
  - Perineural invasion: Present
  - Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified within biopsy material
  - Necrosis: Focal (comedo-type pattern, <10% of tumor area)
  - No morphologic evidence of neuroendocrine transdifferentiation (see ancillary studies)
  - See immunohistochemistry and FISH results below

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION

Received in formalin are two tan-white cylindrical core needle biopsy specimens, each measuring approximately 1.0–1.2 cm in length and 0.1 cm in diameter, consistent with 18-gauge core needle biopsy material. The cores are firm and partially gritty in texture, consistent with osseous/mineralized tissue. Both cores are submitted entirely for histologic processing following standard decalcification. The specimen was submitted in toto in a single cassette labeled A.

Total tissue submitted: 2 cores, entirely submitted in 1 cassette.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION

Sections through the decalcified core needle biopsy material demonstrate markedly hypercellular bone marrow space virtually entirely replaced by infiltrating carcinoma. The neoplastic cells form poorly differentiated infiltrative glands (Gleason pattern 4) with cribriform architecture, as well as sheets and cords of individual cells with marked nuclear pleomorphism (Gleason pattern 5). The individual tumor cells have large vesicular nuclei with prominent macronucleoli, moderate-to-scant cytoplasm, and frequent mitotic figures including atypical forms. Focal comedo-type necrosis is identified within cribriform gland structures, occupying approximately 5–8% of tumor surface area. The surrounding bony trabeculae are focally eroded and remodeled, with reactive fibrosis and scattered osteoclast-type giant cells at the tumor-bone interface. Hematopoietic elements are markedly reduced and largely replaced by tumor.

There is no convincing morphologic evidence of small cell or large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma. No rosette formation, salt-and-pepper chromatin, or neuroendocrine organoid architecture is identified on hematoxylin-and-eosin sections. There are no features of squamous or sarcomatoid differentiation.

Ki-67 proliferation index (MIB-1 clone): Estimated at approximately 35% of viable tumor nuclei (hot-spot analysis).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed in-house; controls show appropriate reactivity):

  PSA (polyclonal):                Positive, diffuse strong cytoplasmic staining (3+)
  NKX3.1 (EP356 clone):           Positive, diffuse strong nuclear staining (3+)
  Androgen Receptor (AR) (SP107 clone): Positive, diffuse strong nuclear staining (3+)
  Prostein (P501S):                Positive, granular cytoplasmic staining (2+)
  PSMA:                           Positive, membranous/cytoplasmic (2+)
  Synaptophysin (SP11 clone):      NEGATIVE
  Chromogranin A (DAK-A3 clone):   NEGATIVE
  CD56 (123C3 clone):              NEGATIVE
  INSM1 (A-8 clone):               NEGATIVE
  p53 (DO-7 clone):                Aberrant overexpression pattern (diffuse strong nuclear, >80% of tumor cells — consistent with gain-of-function TP53 mutation)
  Ki-67 (MIB-1 clone):             Approximately 35% (hot-spot)
  CK8/18 (5D3 clone):              Positive, diffuse (consistent with carcinoma)
  CK20:                            Negative
  CK7:                             Negative
  p40:                             Negative
  TTF-1:                           Negative
  CDX2:                            Negative

Interpretation: The immunophenotype is fully consistent with metastatic prostatic adenocarcinoma. Immunohistochemical markers of neuroendocrine differentiation (synaptophysin, chromogranin A, CD56, INSM1) are ALL NEGATIVE, arguing against overt neuroendocrine transdifferentiation at this time point. Aberrant p53 expression pattern is consistent with the previously identified somatic TP53 p.R248W gain-of-function mutation.

FISH ANALYSIS (Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization):

  BRCA2 (13q12.3) by FISH:
    Probe: BRCA2/CEN13 dual-color FISH probe set
    Result: HOMOZYGOUS DELETION OF BRCA2 CONFIRMED
    Signals per tumor cell (mean, 50 cells counted):
      BRCA2 signal: 0.1 (effectively absent)
      CEN13 (centromere 13 reference) signal: 2.0 (diploid)
    Interpretation: Complete biallelic loss of BRCA2, consistent with loss of heterozygosity (somatic second hit) in the context of known germline BRCA2 pathogenic frameshift variant (c.5946delT). This finding confirms homozygous BRCA2 loss in the metastatic deposit and supports eligibility for PARP inhibitor therapy.

  PTEN (10q23) by FISH (performed given known somatic PTEN loss on prior sequencing):
    Result: Homozygous deletion of PTEN confirmed (PTEN signals absent; CEP10 reference 2.0 copies per cell)

PENDING STUDIES:
  - Somatic next-generation sequencing (NGS) panel (sent to [REFERENCE LABORATORY]): Results pending. Ordered to evaluate for AR amplification, AR ligand-binding domain mutations, RB1 status, and other potential resistance mechanisms. Results to be reported as an addendum.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT

The morphologic and immunohistochemical findings confirm metastatic prostatic adenocarcinoma, Gleason grade group 5, in this CT-guided right iliac crest biopsy specimen. The tumor demonstrates high-grade histologic features including cribriform architecture with comedo necrosis, pattern 5 growth, and a high Ki-67 proliferation index (35%). Importantly, IHC markers of neuroendocrine differentiation are entirely negative on this specimen, including synaptophysin, chromogranin A, CD56, and INSM1, indicating that frank neuroendocrine transdifferentiation (treatment-emergent neuroendocrine prostate cancer) is not present at this biopsy site at this time.

The diffuse androgen receptor (AR) positivity by IHC is noted; this does not exclude the possibility of AR amplification or AR mutation at the DNA/genomic level (which may not be detectable by protein IHC alone), and the pending NGS panel results are expected to provide additional information regarding AR pathway alterations.

Biallelic BRCA2 loss by FISH, combined with the known germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant, confirms eligibility criteria consistent with published PROfound trial inclusion criteria for PARP inhibitor therapy in mCRPC.

Correlation with clinical findings and molecular profiling results (NGS addendum pending) is recommended.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSING PATHOLOGIST: [PATHOLOGIST NAME, MD]
Faculty, Division of Surgical Pathology
Board-Certified, Anatomic and Clinical Pathology

ELECTRONIC SIGNATURE APPLIED

Report Status: FINAL (NGS addendum pending)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
